PREPARED FOR Godwin Bortey

A 2–3 minute cinematic experience that makes investors believe Bridge State University & Bridge City already exist.

SCROLL FOR DETAILS
THE VISION

We’re not selling a school.
We’re selling a world.

The investor should not see a campus — they should see an entire universe capable of supporting multiple seasons, characters, brands, locations and stories. BSU is the dream. Bridge City is the pressure surrounding it. 435 lives where those two realities collide.

To prove the direction before a single frame of video is produced, I’ve already built the visual foundation below — nine cinematic ‘world boards,’ each a complete look at a different district of the universe, fully consistent in branding, color and tone. These become the storyboard the film is built from.

THE WORLD BOARDS

The world, board by board.

01

Founder Square — The Heart of Campus

The prestige pole of the world. Monumental Founder Fountain, clock tower, the black-panther statue and graduation. Establishes BSU as a real, historic institution founded 1961 — the postcard investors remember.

02

Student Life — Culture in Motion

Proof the campus is alive and specific, not stock. Lecture halls, dorms, dining, library study, the roller-skating party, Panther Commons, fashion and a real student ID. The daily energy that makes people say ‘I want to enroll here.’

03

Athletics & Homecoming — The Winning Tradition

Broadcast-scale spectacle and decades of legacy. Night football at Panther Stadium, star player CHARLES #24, marching band, basketball and a Trophy Hall showing history — not one recent win. Sells scale and pride.

04

Greek Row — Omega Gamma Alpha

Brotherhood, rivalry and tradition. Omega Gamma Alpha kept in its own green-and-gold identity, deliberately separate from BSU’s purple/gold/black — step shows, the chapter house, yard shows and regalia. Adds cultural depth.

05

Charles Street — Where Campus Meets the City

The transition zone. Clean campus gives way to older brick storefronts, a barbershop, weathered BSU murals and student-discount signs. Banners thin out and tension quietly rises — the hinge between the two worlds.

06

The Block — Neighborhood Territory

The pressure pole. Cold dusk, the ‘Respect · Loyalty · Power’ mural, a corner store and a local crew watching the block. Restrained and cinematic, never a stereotype — the reality that surrounds the dream.

07

Bridge City Downtown — The City After Class

Ambition and culture after dark. Old factories turned into a music hall, clubs, galleries, podcast studios and a rooftop looking toward the city skyline. Shows the world extends far beyond the campus gates.

08

Wardrobe & Merch — The BSU Apparel System

A monetizable brand identity. Letterman jackets, cardigans, knitwear, hoodies, caps and alumni pieces with consistent embroidery and ‘BSU · 1961’ branding. Signals a franchise that can sell product, not just a school.

09

Campus Icons & Landmarks — The Founders

The institution’s memory. Founders Mary Lou Johnson and Jack Johnson, their bronze statues, the clock tower, administration hall, The Arch, Panther Walk and the cornerstone: ‘Founded 1961 — Bridge the gap among all people.’ This is the history that makes BSU feel real.

10

Seasons at BSU — One School · One City · One Family

Proof of a year-round, multi-season universe. Fall homecoming, winter semester, spring graduation and summer neighborhood nights — closing on the world’s core promise: one school, one city, one family.

PACKAGES

Choose your scope

PACKAGE A

Essential

$2,500
  • Full storyboard (the nine world boards)
  • 2–3 minute cinematic AI world-building film
  • Licensed music track
  • Cinematic color grade
  • 1 revision round
  • 1 delivery format (your choice: 16:9 / 9:16 / 1:1)
  • Custom sound design
  • Voiceover narration
  • Social stills package
PACKAGE B

Signature Recommended

$3,500
  • Everything in Essential, plus:
  • Custom sound design (ambience & effects)
  • Professional voiceover narration
  • Full cinematic color grade
  • Social Stills Package — 25–40 finished images across Campus Icons, Student Life, Athletics, Greek Life, Downtown, Neighborhood, Homecoming & Seasonal (per your Visual Bible).
  • 1 revision round
  • 1 delivery format (your choice)
TERMS

How we work

Payment
  • 50% deposit to start
  • 50% on delivery of the final film
  • All prices in USD
Timeline
  • 10–14 business days for the final film
  • Timing starts once the script and the assets you need to send are received
Revisions
  • 1 revision round included
  • Changes within the approved concept
  • A new concept / direction is quoted separately
Deliverables & Files
  • Final export only — no source project files
  • 1 delivery format of your choice
  • 30-day archive after delivery
BEFORE WE START

A few questions for you

The Visual Bible gives us a rich world. To turn it into a focused 2–3 minute film, I need three things locked before production:

1

A clear script / brief

Please provide a short written brief of exactly what the film should show, and in what order.

2

Are there main characters to create?

Do we need to design recurring hero characters and keep them consistent across shots — for example CHARLES #24, a student lead, or a key figure from The Block? If so, how many characters are needed? This affects the price.

3

What’s the story beyond the world?

Beyond ‘make investors believe this world exists,’ what should they feel walking away? Is there an underlying story — the dream vs. the pressure, a character’s rise, the collision at 435 — that the film should quietly carry?

READY TO BUILD THE WORLD?
— Godwin Bortey
